programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison learns her brain tumour has returned, and tragedy strikes when Joe wakes one morning to find she has died in her sleep. Supernatural drama, starring Patricia Arquette and Sofia Vassilieva

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene discovers the farmhands concocting a virility potion for Rocky the bull, and manages to sneak a dose into Boycie's coffee. Make-up is found in Tyler's room, and his parents are relieved to be told he has a girlfriend - until it transpires she is also his teacher. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Jack Doolan star

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Alzheimer sufferer Edward Alderston comes under suspicion of involvement when an ex-finance company high-flyer responsible for the loss of many elderly people's life savings is found floating in a swimming pool with a samurai sword in his back

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a troubled student at Upcott Seminary leads Father Brown to believe a murderer is at work, and he tries to persuade Sid to go undercover in an attempt to flush out the killer. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: A predator is targeting contestants in a beauty pageant and Brinford's new WPC Annie Taylor is soon on the trail. Meanwhile, there is a shock in store for Briggs and DI Sawyer when old foes and wrong-doings come back to haunt them. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Claudia Jessie and Oliver Rix

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. McVitie suffers a heart attack and Jim is promoted to acting superintendent in his absence, but does not take kindly to the extra responsibility - or being accused of benefiting from someone else's misfortune. Crime drama, featuring the final appearance of Mark McManus as Jim Taggart

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the hospital board meet to make their decision about Ash's future, Suki has one more trick up her sleeve. Sharon's leaving party is unexpectedly quiet, but it proves the perfect opportunity for Kim to sign people up to her dating services. Mitch realises his efforts were in vain as he tries to do the right thing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces a topical debate with a virtual audience from around the UK. On his panel this week are the Safeguarding Minister Victoria Atkins MP, Shadow Minister for Domestic Violence and Safeguarding Jess Phillips MP, Deputy Leader of the SNP at Westminster Kirsten Oswald MP, economist and former Governor of the Bank of Canada and of the Bank of England Mark Carney and journalist and broadcaster Ian Hislop

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit properties in Birkenhead, Paisley and Madeley Heath, Staffordshire, talking to their new owners about their plans

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss offer advice as contestants head out into the pouring rain in Shrewsbury to secure items of potential profit under the gavel, where auctioneer Philip Serrell awaits. Love is in the air for presenter Christina Trevanion as she explains how to flirt using a fan

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Today's buyers live in Whitstable and have £750 to spend on a seaside-themed masterpiece

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor goes rural property-shopping in the Yorkshire Dales with a retired head teacher who has £400,000 to invest in her leap to the country and has signed up her daughter to help her with the decision making. Away from a high-energy house hunt, Margherita discovers how one Dales company has recently been instrumental in reviving Britain's hand-made piano heritage, an echo of production glory days during the 1920s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Married couple Marcus and Paul are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Anna Haugh in the fast-and-furious cookery contest. Paul does most of the cooking in their home and he's keen for Marcus to get more involved. The winner will be decided in a hectic 10-Minute Challenge where the chefs must cook against the clock to produce show stopping dishes under pressure. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Leather expert Suzie Fletcher undertakes the repair of a treasured toy horse, while metal expert Dominic Chinea's knowledge is called upon to rejuvenate a vintage lawnmower. Also receiving attention is a delicately carved set of clay sculptures

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Comic Relief 2021
programme.desc: Lenny Henry, Davina McCall, Paddy McGuinness, Alesha Dixon and David Tennant preside as stars of stage, screen and the movies entertain the nation with hilarious sketches, live performances, big surprises and stunning music acts in a bid to raise much-needed cash for good causes at home and across the world. Highlights include Michael Sheen, Anna Friel and Keira Knightley in the greatest disaster movie never made, the hosts of Top Gear facing interrogation by their most savage critics to date and Dawn French back as Geraldine Granger, joined by the Rev Kate Bottley. Fans of Fleabag and Normal People get a confessional close-up as the stars of two of TV's biggest shows meet for a mash up like no other, Alex Scott and Jermaine Jenas take part in the world's largest and longest Red Nose and Spoon Race and Tim Vine rewards the men and women of the UK's military forces by spreading a smile. Five comedians have just 24 hours to perfect their opera voices under the watchful eye of their mento

programme.title: Comic Relief 2021
programme.desc: Jason Manford and Amanda Holden continue the Comic Relief extravaganza with special guests, comedy, and the chance for viewers to win a number of once-in-a-lifetime prizes. With music by Rag'n'Bone Man, and Joel Corry and RAYE

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo and his son Luci compete in this special edition of the game show, stacking the gold bars on the balance board in a bid to win up to £100,000 for charity. But if they have answered incorrectly, the bars they stack will amount to nothing - and only serve to add further weight to the already precarious board, which could collapse at any second and leave them with nothing

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Sabrina Ghayour, Tom Booton and Richard Curtis. Olly Smith joins by video link, and selects wines to accompany the meals prepared in the studio

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts this celebrity edition of the kitchen duel, which sees actor Stephen Mangan squaring up against barrister and TV judge Robert Rinder in a battle of the pots and pans. Paired with chefs Jeremy Pang and Ellis Barrie, they must create dishes from their £5.00 bag of ingredients in just 20 minutes - and with everything from a spicy noodle soup to a massive mushroom vol-au-vent on offer, it promises to be a close call

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Karen Carney and Dion Dublin to look ahead to the sixth round of the FA Cup

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp challenges two teams to search for bargains at an antiques fair in the grounds of Kedleston Hall, Derbyshire. John Cameron and Tim Weeks offer expert advice

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Scotland have won the last nine meetings between the sides and will expect to extend that run today given their superior form in this year's championship. With analysis from John Barclay and Jeremy Guscott and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Andy Nicol

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites Amanda Abbington, Rick Edwards, Richard Blackwood and Laura Tobin to take to the black chair. In the first round, they are put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - Phoebe from Friends, An American Werewolf in London, Bon Jovi, and the octopus. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts the game show, which sees an engaged couple of teachers from Essex answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck. They hope to put the prize money towards a new home for themselves and their baby daughter

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. Les Bleus clinched a 27-23 victory when the sides met at Principality Stadium last year, but Wales prevailed 24-19 on their last visit here in 2019, and should head into this match in a confident mood after easily beating rivals England 40-24 in the third round of fixtures. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson and Thomas Castaignede, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion v Newcastle United. Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Premier League match at Amex Stadium. Both teams were in need of points to boost their chances of avoiding relegation, and it was Brighton who were comfortable 3-0 victors when the sides met at St James' Park in September. Plus, a look back at Friday's clash between Fulham and Leeds United at Craven Cottage

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from today's sixth-round ties - Bournemouth v Southampton at Vitality Stadium and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park. Bournemouth were the only Championship side remaining in the competition but will have been hoping to make home advantage count against their South Coast rivals, while the Citizens were looking to emulate their 3-1 away win over Everton in the league last month

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts the game show, which sees an engaged couple of teachers from Essex answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck. They hope to put the prize money towards a new home for themselves and their baby daughter

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a dog groomer who wants to relocate her business to Suffolk on a budget of £320,000. He also meets the team behind an ambitious project to restore an Anglo-Saxon longship, the remains of which were unearthed by an amateur archaeologist in the 1930s

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Julie and Debbie are both interested in rustling up healthy meals they can make without spending hours in the kitchen. Chefs Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal makes sure things don't get too boring

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces the unique talents of the world's most intelligent animals, investigating how nature's masterminds measure up against human brain power. Looking at a variety of animals, such as ravens, crows, bees and starlings, Chris explores how different animals can use tools, solve complex puzzles, recognise themselves in mirrors and even reason like humans

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Artist Ricky Martin on creating your own cartoon, plus more Big Songs

programme.title: Charles Dickens and the Invention of Christmas
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones reveals how Charles Dickens virtually invented the idea of a traditional family Christmas through one of his books, A Christmas Carol. When it was published in 1843, the story of Ebenezer Scrooge captured the imagination of Victorian Britain, and boosted the new craze for the festive season at a time when the idea of Santa Claus, mistletoe and crackers were relatively new

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss visit an antiques fair in Waterloo, Belgium, where James splashes out on an expensive portrait and Kate buys a rare toy aeroplane

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss continue their rivalry at an antiques fair in Peterborough, buying and selling collectibles for the most profit

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in St Albans - including a country lodge, a 1970s house, and an ultra-modern family home - are in the spotlight

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team pays a visit to Gloucester Cathedral, where experts Philip Serrell and Michael Baggott discover some rare gems among the antiques and collectibles brought for valuation, including a pre-Columbian burial vessel - one of the oldest items ever to have been featured on the show. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin marvels at the building's stained glass windows, one of which is as big as a tennis court

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and team head to the Forest of Dean, where they help Jon and Michelle to turn their old horse cart into a cosy and comfortable sanctuary for guests

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers sample the delicacies of a fisherwoman who had to fight for a place in the fishing community, before cooking meatballs with wild boar. The duo take DNA tests to find out if they have any Viking blood flowing through their veins, before heading for Stockholm where Dave takes a preserving masterclass with city gardener Johan and Si gets a pastry crash course with one of Sweden's youngest and most talented bakers. They conclude their road trip by taking a ferry to the medieval island of Gotland

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: The Secret Science of Sewage
programme.desc: Dr George McGavin, Dr Zoe Laughlin and a team of world-leading scientists explore the biology, chemistry, natural history and social history of faeces from a TV-base camp like no other - within the pools, tanks and bowels of one of the UK's largest and most technologically advanced sewage works

programme.title: Rob Brydon's Now That's What I Call Comic Relief
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Rob Brydon takes a look at the funniest, sweetest and silliest musical sketches and performances from the Comic Relief store cupboard

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: New discoveries from the edge of the solar system are transforming what is known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close up images of the planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano, with data sent back lead some scientists to speculate that there may even be life on Pluto today

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. In the first episode, she comes to the aid of Richard, who wants to learn if a man claiming to be his biological father is the genuine article, and Charlie, who wants to know what her chances are of contracting the same terminal illness as her father

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the four talented heat-winners return to fight for a place in knockout week in the Critic's Test. Their brief - set by Grace Dent, one of the country's toughest food critics - is to cook their take on a great British pudding. Presented by Gregg Wallace and John Torode

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: In a special programme to end this series, Mary is in conversation with British writer Jed Mercurio. He's the mastermind behind hit show Line of Duty, returning for a new series in the spring, and has a rich back catalogue of attention-grabbing TV drama including Bodyguard, Cardiac Arrest and Bodies. How much does his early medical and military training inform his writing? And how much drama does it take to make real events into unmissable television?

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts look around properties in Blackpool, Derby and the West Midlands town of Bilston, before hearing how they have been redeveloped

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glasgow friends Simon and Carolyn are the chief cooks in both their households. They know each other's strengths and weaknesses well and are keen to see whether that will help or hinder them in Rylan Clark-Neal's fast-and-furious kitchen contest. They are joined in the Green Kitchen by queen of spice Romy Gill, while Anna Haugh brings a modern British and Irish twist to the Red Kitchen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Ben Cajee and Katie Thistleton present Creativity Week, with Pixie Lott in the Big Song

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into another seven dishes, demonstrating how, by experimenting, it is possible to create foods to suit different moods. Dishes include duck and citrus salad, hot yoghurt steak and chocolate beetroot cake

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in St Albans - a converted farmyard barn with a modern twist, a zero-carbon self-build and a 1950s Scandinavian-style bungalow

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to St Albans, Hertfordshire, to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Adam Partridge and Caroline Hawley inspect interesting antiques brought in by the public at Hopetoun House near Edinburgh, and presenter Paul Martin discovers more about the property by talking to those who live and work there

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, as a House of Champions winner is crowned

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team travel to Hampshire to meet Kerie and Dave, to transform an abandoned shepherd's hut and pigsty into a unique B&B

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the Cairngorm Mountain Rescue Team, with leader Willie Anderson and his 50-strong crew of volunteers saving lives in the region. Covering 2,000 sq miles of one Britain's wildest landscapes, they battle blizzards, avalanches and 100mph winds as they respond to emergencies

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Journalist Adrian Chiles, former politician Edwina Currie, Olympian Fatima Whitbread, broadcaster Mim Shaikh, TV presenter Amar Latif, comedian Dom Joly and actress Pauline McLynn set out to cover sections of the Sultans Trail - a modern-day 2,200km pilgrimage across Eastern Europe that starts in Vienna and ends in Istanbul. Each has varying religions, with Dom and Pauline confirmed atheists. Starting in Serbia's capital Belgrade, they stay in basic hostels and sleep in shared dorms, acting as simple pilgrims traversing the ancient military route by rail and foot

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don welcomes spring at Longmeadow with plenty of changes in the garden to share. As well as getting on with some essential pruning, he lifts and divides perennials, starts sowing chillies and begins growing this year's vegetables. Plus, Carol Klein shares the highlights of the plants that shine out in winter at Bressingham Gardens in Norfolk

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The presenter chats to actor, comedian and music lover Lenny Henry, who chooses and discusses his favourite performances from the Later archive. Plus, two artists make their debut on the show. First up is Enny, a rapper and singer from Thamesmead in south east London. And Leeds-London collective Nubiyan Twist give a unique performance of their latest single, If I Know

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Faisal Islam

programme.title: Maps to the Stars (2014)
programme.desc: A disfigured woman is hired as an assistant to a fading Hollywood star, who has become obsessed with her dead mother and is determined to recreate her most famous film role. Meanwhile, a teen idol goes off the rails under the influence of his overbearing parents and a chauffeur harbours dreams of becoming a writer. David Cronenberg's drama, starring Julianne Moore, Mia Wasikowska, John Cusack and Robert Pattinson

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: When Baltar's representatives experience an assassination attempt Admiral Adama asks Lee to offer them protection, who attempts to play an active role in defending the prisoner during his assignment

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As the government faces mounting criticism that well-connected people made millions out of Britain's PPE crisis, Panorama investigates who won out. More than £12billion was spent in the first six months of the pandemic on contracts to provide personal protective equipment. Reporter Richard Bilton meets one man who made £40million on a deal, and speaks to others who felt ignored in favour of less experienced suppliers. As the government refuses to reveal the full details of all its so-called VIP deals, the programme reveals the high-profile connections to one lucrative contract

programme.title: Stonehenge: The Lost Circle Revealed
programme.desc: Using cutting-edge research, a dedicated team of archaeologists has compiled the evidence to fill in a 400-year gap in man's knowledge of the bluestones of Stonehenge. By combining innovative 3D scanning techniques, traditional field archaeology and novel laboratory analysis, the team has discovered where the stones were quarried and where they first stood. Professor Alice Roberts shows how the team made its discoveries and joins Professor Mike Parker Pearson to piece together the final parts of the puzzle, solving one of the toughest challenges ever faced by archaeologists

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out some mind-boggling armies from the natural world, including some sleepy suburbs invaded by 10 million bugs

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Two Sherlock fans, sisters Mille and Leila, plan a wedding mystery, featuring bangs, crashes and exploding balloons as their parents try to solve 'the case of the missing rings'

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Two brothers want a den that looks like a Scottish castle

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Viewers do something to Share a Smile all over the UK

programme.title: America's Cup
programme.desc: Action from the latest round of the 36th America's Cup, as Emirates Team New Zealand and Luna Rossa Challenge compete for the famous trophy

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Invisible Worlds
programme.desc: Richard examines some of the hidden wonders that lie beyond the limits of the naked eye. In the first programme, he explores the world of hidden detail during the time it takes the human eye to blink, and uses high-speed cameras to glimpse one of nature's most fleeting spectacles

programme.title: Me and My Dog: The Ultimate Contest
programme.desc: Chris Packham hosts the show where dog owners and their pets compete in physical and mental challenges designed to test their ability to communicate, with trainers and canine scientists on hand to help them develop the relationship between human and animal. In the first episode, half the contestants must lead their dogs through a series of gates using verbal and physical signals, while the others must collect tennis balls in fast-flowing water. The final challenge sees the owners and dogs compete in a tethered biking challenge, to decide who has triumphed in the first stage of the contest

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don journeys to northern Europe, where an abundance of botanical hot spots reveals a culture obsessed with creating gardens. He discovers the floral splendour of Britain's Sissinghurst Castle and the much painted garden of impressionist Monet at Giverny, before witnessing the midnight sun in Norway as he discovers how plants behave 200 miles above the Arctic Circle

programme.title: The Chronicles of Nadiya
programme.desc: Part two of two. After spending a week with her family in Sylhet, Nadiya Hussain cooks her way around Bangladesh and learns more about the country, its people and its food. In the capital city Dhaka, the Great British Bake Off winner spends time with a charity that delivers school meals to deprived children, before learning about an ancient fishing method in a small Hindu village on the banks of the Padma. As her journey continues, she meets and learns from food entrepreneurs and experiences a side of modern Bangladesh she never knew existed

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Simon King travel the UK in search of quality baked British staples - pies, pastries and cakes. They begin by looking at bread and suggesting inexpensive ideas for home baking. Among the recipes they demonstrate are a classic brown loaf, a brown-ale and cheese bread to serve with onion soup, and naan to accompany a curry prepared on the shores of Derbyshire's Derwent reservoir

programme.title: Meet Me in St Louis (1944)
programme.desc: A job offer tempts a banker to consider uprooting his close-knit family and moving to New York. However, his wife and children struggle with the prospect of leaving their friends behind, and are horrified when they realise they might miss out on the 1904 St Louis World's Fair. Meanwhile, teenage daughter Esther faces the abrupt termination of her romance with the boy next door. Musical, starring Judy Garland, Tom Drake and Margaret O'Brien

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin, Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey head to Dartmoor, where items of interest include a platinum disc awarded for Coldplay's 2005 album X&Y, which is signed by the band's members. Plus, an expert in America talks about a walking stick that is associated with General Tom Thumb

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: A journey along the American river, whose fingers stretch into nearly half of the USA, collecting water from 31 states. The documentary follows the Mississippi through varied landscapes from its source to its mouth, starting in the towering Rocky Mountains of Wyoming and Montana, where billions of tons of water, ultimately destined to flow south are trapped as ice and snow

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, triplets Peter, David and Phillip, who were adopted as young boys, have their DNA analysed by Professor Turi King as they attempt to explore their birth father's heritage. Meanwhile, a woman who was conceived from a sperm donor longs to learn about the other half of her ancestry, and find out if she has any half donor siblings

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As semi-final week kicks off, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two restaurants in Hebden Bridge. Judge Michelle Ogundehin reveals that the competitors will be working in pairs, with each designer responsible for a single floor in the restaurant. Who will make it to the final?

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian continues his personal review of the 1980s, considering how by the middle of the decade, Britain was wrestling with two contradictory impulses - the rise of a strong nationalist sentiment and the emergence of an increasingly globalised world. Along the way Sandbrook examines everything from the invasion of the Falkland Islands to the invasion of the home computer. He examines the moral panic surrounding video nasties, looks at the Americanisation of UK culture and the picket-line skirmishes playing nightly on TV, as well as the Aids epidemic and the threat of the IRA

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: 1985 sees the 'Big Four' of 80's Pop - Spandau, Wham, Culture Club and the Durans - have a relatively quiet year which ushers in a new generation of pop talent. The show becomes a barometer for the state of the nation as the ambitious King and A-ha, propelled by the most expensive video of the year for Take On Me, compete for attention with passionate politicos Billy Bragg and The Style Council who, fired up by the ongoing miners' strike and continuing threat of nuclear war, give the studio audience their all with Between The Wars and Walls Come Tumbling Down. 1985 also sees electro dance hit the mainstream as Dead or Alive top the charts with You Spin Me Round, the first number one for producers Stock, Aitken and Waterman, and Paul Hardcastle holds the top slot for five weeks with 19, a spoken word single made with new sampling technology and released on the 10th anniversary of the end of the Vietnam war. Meanwhile, movie tie-ins and record companies eyeing a 'housewives' market see a

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks a completely random selection of questions this week, with Bill Bailey, Daliso Chaponda, Sally Phillips and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: My Father and Me ()
programme.desc: Documentary maker Nick Broomfield takes a distinctly personal look at his relationship with his humanist-pacifist father Maurice, a factory worker turned photographer of vivid images of postwar England. These images inspired Nick's own film-making career, but also spoke to a difference in outlook between the pair

programme.title: Love & Mercy (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of the Beach Boys' Brian Wilson, following the singer-songwriter at two periods in his life. In the 1960s, he is at his creative peak, but haunted by inner demons that take their toll on his mental health. By the 1980s, he is a broken man dominated by a shady doctor, until a new relationship offers him a chance at recovery. Starring John Cusack, Paul Dano, Paul Giamatti and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: The Commune (2016)
programme.desc: A couple establish a commune in a large house in 1970s Copenhagen, encouraging anyone who wants to escape the outside world to join them and their daughter. A succession of unusual characters pass through the collective, but their supposed utopia looks set to be torn apart by an affair within the group. Drama, starring Ulrich Thomsen and Fares Fares. In Danish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge discovers the secret role that neutral Ireland played assisting the Allies in the Second World War

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which adventurer Paul Rose explores the 79 miles of the Yorkshire Wolds Way, one of Britain's least well-known national walking trails. The trail starts at the Humber Estuary and ends at the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Filey. On the way, Paul takes in the views from the top of the Humber Bridge, learns to ride a penny farthing and searches out the spots made internationally famous by the artist David Hockney

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: As night settles over a rocky coast, Bob Ross produces a warm glow in the sky in a painting on black canvas

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's luck takes a sudden turn for the better in his romance with Helen, but there is no respite for the unfortunate Dalby family. Tristan's capacity for causing chaos shows no sign of coming to an end

programme.title: My Feral Heart (2016)
programme.desc: Following his mother's death, Luke, a young man with Down's syndrome, moves into a care home. After sneaking out of the house to explore the countryside, he meets a young woman who gives him hope and purpose. Drama, starring Darren Kent, Steven Brandon, Suzanna Hamilton and Pixie Le Knot

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines the coming-of-age film, and explains why it is such a powerful and popular medium for exploring the tumult and transformations of adolescence. Featured movies include The 400 Blows, The Breakfast Club, Kes and Lady Bird

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens reveals how, as the 20th century dawned, artists found radical new ways to depict Wales, pushing themselves to extremes and redefining what art could be. His venture takes him to the National Museum Wales in Cardiff, Bardsey Island off the Llyn Peninsula, Colwyn Bay and Swansea

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank's journey takes him through Uzbekistan, Iran and Syria, where he finds the local people far more welcoming to Western travellers than expected. Among the locations he visits are the Fire Temple of Azerbaijan, the Trading Domes of Uzbekistan, the Bisitun carvings at the top of a cliff face in Iran, and the ancient city of Persepolis

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: As night settles over a rocky coast, Bob Ross produces a warm glow in the sky in a painting on black canvas

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which adventurer Paul Rose explores the 79 miles of the Yorkshire Wolds Way, one of Britain's least well-known national walking trails. The trail starts at the Humber Estuary and ends at the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Filey. On the way, Paul takes in the views from the top of the Humber Bridge, learns to ride a penny farthing and searches out the spots made internationally famous by the artist David Hockney

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines the coming-of-age film, and explains why it is such a powerful and popular medium for exploring the tumult and transformations of adolescence. Featured movies include The 400 Blows, The Breakfast Club, Kes and Lady Bird

programme.title: Folk Hibernia at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the finest in Irish folk music, featuring a compilation of performances from the BBC archives. Highlights include songs by the Clancy Brothers, the Chieftains, Christy Moore, the Pogues and Sharon Shannon

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on October 18, with performances by artists including A-ha, Aztec Camera and Mick Jones, Innocence, Whitney Houston, Happy Mondays, Rita MacNeil, Hi Tek 3 featuring Ya Kid K, Maria McKee and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on October 25, with performances by artists including Belinda Carlisle, Public Image Ltd, the Beautiful South, Berlin, Paul McCartney, the Righteous Brothers, the Soup Dragons, Paul Simon, Roxette and Jason Donovan

programme.title: St Patrick's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of classic pop performances from the BBC archives to celebrate St Patrick's Day. This selection reflects the huge impact that music from Ireland and Northern Ireland has made across the globe, with performances from U2, Sinead O'Connor, Van Morrison, the Pogues, the Corrs, the Cranberries, Boyzone and Westlife

programme.title: Citizens of Boomtown: The Story of the Boomtown Rats
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the Irish band led by Bob Geldof, who scored hits in the late 1970s and early 80s with the likes of Rat Trap and I Don't Like Mondays, before disbanding a year after Geldof's hugely successful Live Aid event in 1985. The group reformed in 2013 and released their seventh studio album in March 2020

programme.title: The Irish Rock Story: A Tale of Two Cities
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how rock music evolved in Ireland over 40 years. The film demonstrates how the sounds that emerged were grounded in the musical traditions of the island's two main cities, from the pioneering touring showbands of the late 1950s through to the modern day. Richard Dormer narrates an examination of how the harder sounds of Belfast merged with the more folk-centric traditions of Dublin, celebrates some of the key musicians to have emerged from the Irish scene, and reveals how they were influenced by the political, social and cultural climate of their surroundings. With contributions from U2, Sinead O'Connor and Bob Geldof

programme.title: Showbands: How Ireland Learnt to Party
programme.desc: The showband was a uniquely Irish phenomenon. It was a movement that saw thousands of young people travel up and down the country in the late '50s, '60s and early '70s to the 'Dancehalls of Romance' to be entertained by the Royal Showband, the Miami Showband and Big Tom and the Mainliners, and individuals including Dickie Rock, Joe Dolan and Brendan Bowyer. Ardal O'Hanlon looks back at the phenomenon, examining what triggered the infamous era, the people involved, and its eventual end in the 1980s

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright narrates a St Patrick's Day special, featuring archive performances by the Boomtown Rats, the Corrs, Enya, Shane MacGowan and Johnny Depp, Stiff Little Fingers, Thin Lizzy, Sinead O'Connor and U2

programme.title: St Patrick's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of classic pop performances from the BBC archives to celebrate St Patrick's Day. This selection reflects the huge impact that music from Ireland and Northern Ireland has made across the globe, with performances from U2, Sinead O'Connor, Van Morrison, the Pogues, the Corrs, the Cranberries, Boyzone and Westlife

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to find a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety

programme.title: Land of Mine (2015)
programme.desc: In post-Second World War Denmark, a group of young German POWs are forced to clear a beach of thousands of landmines under the watch of a Danish sergeant who slowly learns to appreciate their plight. Drama, starring Roland Moller, Louis Hofmann and Joel Basman. In German, Danish and English

programme.title: Double Agent: The Eddie Chapman Story
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre revisits the story of wartime double agent Eddie Chapman, the subject matter of his best-selling book Agent Zigzag. He reveals how, during the Second World War, Chapman was able to dupe Nazi Germany so successfully that he was awarded the Iron Cross, the country's most prestigious decoration. Includes footage from an interview the former spy gave three years before his death in 1997

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers

programme.desc: Mimi’s boyfriend, James, decides to take her on a date. She prepares for what she thinks is a romantic picnic and enlists help from CBBC’s Nikki Lilly to help bake some cupcakes for dessert. However, the date doesn’t quite go as planned as James surprises Mimi with a visit to a petting zoo. Mimi is encouraged to hold a reptile that she’s most terrified of – a snake! She manages to get James back by finding a Mexican red-knee tarantula for him to hold, but his reaction is a bit more dramatic than she anticipates. We also meet Amira and her sisters, who cook a romantic dinner for their parents - the pressure is on for them to get their nan’s favourite Jamaican dish just right!

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: Mimi is on a mission to debunk stereotypes by showing that you don’t have to be just one thing in life. She celebrates people who inspire her by following their dreams and doing what they love. One of those people is Tesha, an old school friend who runs a salon, loves hair and make-up, but also plays football in the FA Women’s National League and internationally. We also meet Nora, who has albinism and is living her dream as a fashion model. And Mimi hosts her Q&A with SBTV creator Jamal Edwards MBE, who shares his own journey to success, and how he has helped the likes of Ed Sheeran. Mimi’s family and friends make a surprise appearance for the end of the series and offer words of wisdom for anyone wanting to chase their own dreams.

A chance sighting in a fashion magazine introduces him to a whole new world of colour and creativity. But can a boy really wear a dress - and what will his headmaster, dad and friends in the football team think if they find out? Billy Kennedy stars as Dennis, with a supporting cast including Jennifer Saunders, Tim McInnerny, Meera Syal, Steve Speirs, Felicity Montagu, James Buckley and Walliams himself, with a cameo appearance by supermodel Kate Moss

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Jas decides to follow in her dad's footsteps and try out for the junior hockey team. Mr Salford was a legendary hockey goalie in his time at the school. When the try-outs are cancelled due to the coach not being available, Mr Salford steps in and offers his services as coach. Mrs Griggs is horrified - she could do without the chaos he inevitably brings with him - but he won't take no for an answer. Jas and her father are really superstitious and when things start going wrong Jas thinks she's jinxed, and then that she's passed on the jinx to her dad. When she's not picked as goalie for the team Jas is actually relieved - it must mean she's still jinxed and hasn't passed on the jinx to her dad. Eventually Jas does need to play in goal, but when she saves a crucial penalty she and her dad are delighted to realise they are not jinxed after all, just accident-prone! programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mrs Griggs decides that a 'social skills' class could be beneficial for certain pupils - including Martha, Rufus, Cassie and Maxwell. When Mrs Griggs tells Martha a winning personality as well as academic achievement is vital in order to get into a top university, Martha panics and is determined to succeed in the social skills class and prove she does indeed have a winning personality. programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Emily and Matthew as they discover the world around them at five months old and get stronger every day. Emily has a fun day exploring the great outdoors with her brother, sister, mum and dad. They start their day by playing with a ball, rolling it backwards and forwards between them. Emily even manages to push it away too! Meanwhile, Matthew and his family go to a local play cafe where they have fun in a colourful ball pit. Once Emily's family head out for a walk, they find a beautiful waterfall and stream and decide to have a boat race - but who will come out as the winner? When Matthew arrives at a park, he gets the chance to ride a steam train and find out how they make it go! We then follow Emily as she attends a Baby yoga class with her mum and sister. They learn how to strengthen those baby muscles before singing some songs and bouncing on a giant yoga ball. Matthew finishes his day by going down to the beach to help his siblings build a sandcastle.

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. After a tropical rain during the night at Safari World, Andy has been making sure all the animals are safe. Apart from being a little damp, thankfully they’re all okay. Mr Hammond calls Andy and Jen worried that slugs might be eating the giant lettuce he is growing specially for the Galapagos tortoises. Jen and Andy check the cameras and thankfully the slugs have not got to the lettuce – well, not yet at any rate. Jen knows that putting broken shells on the soil around plants deters slugs and snails from eating them. She then discovers on the park’s database that a fish called a tusk fish that has a collection of broken shells. The only problem is, there are no tusk fish at Safari World. They say goodbye to their friends Sid the Snail, Charlie the Spider and Fred the Pill Millipede, before heading out to explore pastures new. But the garden is a big place. They bump into Jago the Robin and Brie the Bumblebee to see if they can help. Can Betty find anywhere suitable?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join six-month-old Erin and Leo as they discover the world around them and start trying new foods for the first time. At home, we meet Erin and her family as everyone is playing and pretending to make phone calls on bananas. Meanwhile, Leo is playing on the swings at the local park, but soon it's time for his daily feed, while his sister stops to enjoy a delicious gingerbread man. After a short picnic, the family feeds the ducks and geese before Leo tries a rice cake for the very first time. At the petting farm, Erin and her family get the chance to feed some goats and hold a baby chick. The family have a picnic with juicy oranges and scrumptious sandwiches, while Erin has a bottle of milk. Leo’s family head home, where mum and Charlotte make a tasting platter for Leo, filled with fresh foods and a yummy yoghurt! At the dinner table, Erin’s mum dishes up a tasty meal - but will she like it?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hannah's family is from South Korea, and she will be making a Korean salmon marinade, which is a popular recipe used there. She starts by adding garlic puree, Korean chilli paste, soy sauce, honey, toasted sesame oil and her special ingredient, sesame seeds, into a jug and mixing them all up. Next, Hannah puts pieces of her salmon onto individual baking sheets and spoons the marinade on. She garnishes them with freshly chopped spring onions, and then her salmon is ready to be cooked in the oven. Hannah wears a colourful Korean outfit and treats her friends to some local Korean kimchi, but will her pals be able to guess her special ingredient?

Jen checks the park database, which has lots of information about African elephants, but it doesn’t say at what age they learn to swim. programme.desc: Lola's family are from Spain, a country in southern Europe. She is making a traditional Spanish dish called empanadas for her and her friends. Lola begins by rolling out two pieces of pizza dough into big circles. She cooks one in the oven. Lola adds tomato puree to passata to make the filling. She also adds some canned tuna, green olives, and boiled egg. She seasons the mixture with paprika, black pepper and her special ingredient, oregano. Then Lola spreads the filling over the dough, which has been in the oven. She finishes the recipe by adding the last piece of dough on top and then putting it back in the oven to cook. Will Lola's friends find the empanadas tasty? Will they guess her special ingredient? There's a very exciting Spanish surprise waiting for her friends while they enjoy their meal!

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces a topical debate with a virtual audience from around the UK. On his panel this week are the Safeguarding Minister Victoria Atkins MP, Shadow Minister for Domestic Violence and Safeguarding Jess Phillips MP, Deputy Leader of the SNP at Westminster Kirsten Oswald MP, economist and former Governor of the Bank of Canada and of the Bank of England Mark Carney and journalist and broadcaster Ian Hislop

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss offer advice as contestants head out into the pouring rain in Shrewsbury to secure items of potential profit under the gavel, where auctioneer Philip Serrell awaits. Love is in the air for presenter Christina Trevanion as she explains how to flirt using a fan

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. Today's buyers live in Whitstable and have £750 to spend on a seaside-themed masterpiece

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Married couple Marcus and Paul are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Anna Haugh in the fast-and-furious cookery contest. Paul does most of the cooking in their home and he's keen for Marcus to get more involved. The winner will be decided in a hectic 10-Minute Challenge where the chefs must cook against the clock to produce show stopping dishes under pressure. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Leather expert Suzie Fletcher undertakes the repair of a treasured toy horse, while metal expert Dominic Chinea's knowledge is called upon to rejuvenate a vintage lawnmower. Also receiving attention is a delicately carved set of clay sculptures

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Comic Relief 2021
programme.desc: Lenny Henry, Davina McCall, Paddy McGuinness, Alesha Dixon and David Tennant preside as stars of stage, screen and the movies entertain the nation with hilarious sketches, live performances, big surprises and stunning music acts in a bid to raise much-needed cash for good causes at home and across the world. Highlights include Michael Sheen, Anna Friel and Keira Knightley in the greatest disaster movie never made, the hosts of Top Gear facing interrogation by their most savage critics to date and Dawn French back as Geraldine Granger, joined by the Rev Kate Bottley. Fans of Fleabag and Normal People get a confessional close-up as the stars of two of TV's biggest shows meet for a mash up like no other, Alex Scott and Jermaine Jenas take part in the world's largest and longest Red Nose and Spoon Race and Tim Vine rewards the men and women of the UK's military forces by spreading a smile. Five comedians have just 24 hours to perfect their opera voices under the watchful eye of their mento

programme.title: Comic Relief 2021
programme.desc: Jason Manford and Amanda Holden continue the Comic Relief extravaganza with special guests, comedy, and the chance for viewers to win a number of once-in-a-lifetime prizes. With music by Rag'n'Bone Man, and Joel Corry and RAYE

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo and his son Luci compete in this special edition of the game show, stacking the gold bars on the balance board in a bid to win up to £100,000 for charity. But if they have answered incorrectly, the bars they stack will amount to nothing - and only serve to add further weight to the already precarious board, which could collapse at any second and leave them with nothing

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Sabrina Ghayour, Tom Booton and Richard Curtis. Olly Smith joins by video link, and selects wines to accompany the meals prepared in the studio

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts this celebrity edition of the kitchen duel, which sees actor Stephen Mangan squaring up against barrister and TV judge Robert Rinder in a battle of the pots and pans. Paired with chefs Jeremy Pang and Ellis Barrie, they must create dishes from their £5.00 bag of ingredients in just 20 minutes - and with everything from a spicy noodle soup to a massive mushroom vol-au-vent on offer, it promises to be a close call

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Karen Carney and Dion Dublin to look ahead to the sixth round of the FA Cup

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas gear up for the Six Nations as Wales travel to face France in the deciding round of fixtures. Their guests are Welsh hero Shane Williams, World Cup-winner Maggie Alphonsi, Paralympic legend Tanni Grey-Thompson and French fly-half Thomas Castaignede

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Scotland have won the last nine meetings between the sides and will expect to extend that run today given their superior form in this year's championship. With analysis from John Barclay and Jeremy Guscott and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Andy Nicol

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites Amanda Abbington, Rick Edwards, Richard Blackwood and Laura Tobin to take to the black chair. In the first round, they are put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - Phoebe from Friends, An American Werewolf in London, Bon Jovi, and the octopus. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts the game show, which sees an engaged couple of teachers from Essex answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck. They hope to put the prize money towards a new home for themselves and their baby daughter

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. Les Bleus clinched a 27-23 victory when the sides met at Principality Stadium last year, but Wales prevailed 24-19 on their last visit here in 2019, and should head into this match in a confident mood after easily beating rivals England 40-24 in the third round of fixtures. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson and Thomas Castaignede, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion v Newcastle United. Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Premier League match at Amex Stadium. Both teams were in need of points to boost their chances of avoiding relegation, and it was Brighton who were comfortable 3-0 victors when the sides met at St James' Park in September. Plus, a look back at Friday's clash between Fulham and Leeds United at Craven Cottage

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from today's sixth-round ties - Bournemouth v Southampton at Vitality Stadium and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park. Bournemouth were the only Championship side remaining in the competition but will have been hoping to make home advantage count against their South Coast rivals, while the Citizens were looking to emulate their 3-1 away win over Everton in the league last month

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts the game show, which sees an engaged couple of teachers from Essex answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck. They hope to put the prize money towards a new home for themselves and their baby daughter

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in in Guildford, Torquay and Barry, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and finds out how the village of Portmeirion became the birthplace of some famous pottery

programme.title: First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Kirsten Campbell presents live coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel has a final chance to persuade Maisie to end the hunger strike, as Jimmi finds out some disturbing news about the university. Luca tries to help two sisters who are at odds over how to care for their father as he struggles with worsening dementia

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is on a property-hunting mission spanning Somerset and Devon's Exmoor National Park with a couple who have a £550,000 budget and a love of carpentry and the coast. Along the way, he visits the Wool Marketing Board's fleece-sorting depot in South Molton, which grades and sells sheep wool from across the south-west region, and which contributes to Britain's £35million wool industry each year

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glasgow friends Simon and Carolyn are the chief cooks in both their households. They know each other's strengths and weaknesses well and are keen to see whether that will help or hinder them in Rylan Clark-Neal's fast-and-furious kitchen contest. They are joined in the Green Kitchen by queen of spice Romy Gill, while Anna Haugh brings a modern British and Irish twist to the Red Kitchen

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher takes on an ornate but broken clock, while metal expert Dominic Chinea tries to rejuvenate a toy carriage dating from 1911. Silversmith Brenton West carefully restores two platinum framed records given to rock star Jimmy McCulloch, a guitarist in Paul McCartney's band Wings

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the hospital board meet to make their decision about Ash's future, Suki has one more trick up her sleeve. Sharon's leaving party is unexpectedly quiet, but it proves the perfect opportunity for Kim to sign people up to her dating services. Mitch realises his efforts were in vain as he tries to do the right thing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The inside story of the most sensational break-up in recent political history. Once close friends and allies, Alex Salmond and Nicola Sturgeon took the SNP from nowhere to government, and Scotland to the brink of independence. Now sworn enemies, former First Minister Salmond accuses some of those he once led of plotting to have him imprisoned. Reporter Mark Daly investigates and asks what the split could mean for the future of Scotland, and for the United Kingdom

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces a topical debate with a virtual audience from around the UK. On his panel this week are the Safeguarding Minister Victoria Atkins MP, Shadow Minister for Domestic Violence and Safeguarding Jess Phillips MP, Deputy Leader of the SNP at Westminster Kirsten Oswald MP, economist and former Governor of the Bank of Canada and of the Bank of England Mark Carney and journalist and broadcaster Ian Hislop

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective
